issues.tradition_claim: The verse denies both agency and patiency to the embodied. Shankara reads this nondually (the Self is neither doer nor done-to). Ramanuja reads this localised (the jīva is not the bodily-act doer; the body's destruction does not destroy the jīva).
issues.engineering_claim: Both fear and guilt around deprecation rest on a category mistake: the spec is not slain when the implementation is.
issues.explanation: The engineering claim is a register translation of the operational consequence both bhasyas share. The category-mistake framing preserves the verse's logical structure (joint negation, not one-sided denial). The analog does not collapse into 'detachment from outcomes' — it specifically targets the symmetric attribution error. The chapter thesis demands the substrate doctrine not be flattened into 'embrace change'; the deprecation analog respects this by retaining the operational duty (migration paths, communication, downstream care). No distortion. PASS.
